contest.title: Codeforces Round 1092 (Unrated, Div. 1, Based on THUPC 2026 Finals)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: maximize non-overlapping harmonious triangles among points with distinct x and distinct y.
Used to confirm the planar-graph upper bound via Euler formula and the two-monotonic-stack construction.
Luogu article cross-checks an equivalent x-sorted version of the monotone-stack construction.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: Given points with all x and y coordinates distinct, construct the maximum number of pairwise interior-disjoint harmonious triangles.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 2e5 and output size <= 2n, so sorting plus linear stack operations is intended.
annotation.core_idea: View the selected triangles as a planar triangulation-like graph. If x is the number of points on the outer face and t is the triangle count, Euler's formula gives t <= 2(n-1)-x. The construction attains this bound: sort points by one coordinate and maintain two opposite monotone stacks. Whenever inserting a point breaks a stack, pop the top and output the triangle formed by the new point, the popped point, and the new stack top. The two stacks cover both monotonic directions, and emitted triangles are harmonious and interior-disjoint.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) for sorting plus O(n) stack operations.
tricks: Euler formula gives the triangle-count upper bound
two opposite monotone stacks emit all triangles
x-sorted and y-sorted versions are coordinate-transposed

solution_variants.name: two-monotone-stacks-construction
solution_variants.summary: Sort by one coordinate, keep one stack increasing and another decreasing in the other coordinate. When the new point violates a stack, pop and output a triangle with the new point, popped point, and previous stack top.

tags.evidence: The task asks for an optimal constellation, and the solution explicitly constructs all triangles by sorted insertion and stack pops.
The triangle set is generated by maintaining two opposite monotone stacks; every pop may emit one triangle.
The optimality proof models the selected triangles as a planar graph and uses Euler's formula.
The validity of triangles depends on axis-aligned rectangle boundary geometry.
During sorted insertion, the algorithm immediately emits triangles for all popped stack elements and still attains the global bound.
Sorting by one coordinate is required before the monotone-stack sweep.
